headline: The United Nations has 193 member states as of 2024.
explanation: The UN currently recognizes 193 member states. Two observer states (Vatican City and Palestine) are not full members.

headline: Vaccines have eradicated smallpox globally.
explanation: Smallpox was officially declared eradicated in 1980 — the first disease eliminated by vaccination.

headline: The Internet was invented in the 1960s as a US military project.
explanation: ARPANET, the predecessor to the Internet, was funded by the US Department of Defense in 1969.

headline: Antarctica is a continent covered by a thick ice sheet.
explanation: Antarctica holds about 90% of the world's ice and 70% of its fresh water.

headline: Every country except the US has ratified the UN Convention on the Rights of the Child.
explanation: The UNCRC is the most widely ratified human rights treaty — 196 countries are party to it.

headline: Climate change is causing the Arctic to warm faster than the rest of Earth.
explanation: Arctic amplification means the Arctic warms 2–4× faster than the global average.

headline: The European Union has 27 member states after Brexit.
explanation: The UK formally left the EU in January 2020, leaving 27 members.

headline: Handwashing with soap can reduce diarrheal disease by nearly 40%.
explanation: Handwashing is one of the most cost-effective public health interventions.

headline: The Universal Declaration of Human Rights was adopted in 1948.
explanation: The UN General Assembly adopted the UDHR on December 10, 1948 — celebrated as Human Rights Day.

headline: The ozone layer has been gradually recovering since the Montreal Protocol.
explanation: The 1987 Montreal Protocol phased out ozone-depleting substances; recovery is on track for mid-century.

headline: Women voted in Saudi Arabia for the first time in 2015.
explanation: Saudi women voted and stood as candidates in municipal elections for the first time in December 2015.

headline: Regular physical exercise can reduce symptoms of depression.
explanation: Exercise is recommended as a first-line treatment for mild-to-moderate depression alongside therapy.

headline: The Berlin Wall fell in November 1989.
explanation: East Germany opened its borders on November 9, 1989, leading to the wall's dismantling.

headline: Only three countries have not officially adopted the metric system.
explanation: The US, Liberia, and Myanmar are the only countries that have not officially adopted the metric system.

headline: Voter turnout tends to be higher in elections held on weekends or public holidays.
explanation: Studies show weekend voting tends to increase turnout by reducing scheduling barriers.

headline: Scientists have confirmed that humans only use 10% of their brains.
explanation: This is a popular myth. Brain scans show that virtually all parts of the brain are active throughout the day.

headline: Drinking eight glasses of water a day is required by law in most countries.
explanation: The '8 glasses a day' rule is a myth — hydration needs vary per person. No country mandates water intake.

headline: The United Nations was founded in 1900 to prevent World War I.
explanation: The UN was founded in 1945, after World War II, not in 1900. The League of Nations (1920) preceded it.

headline: A single social media post can legally override a government election result.
explanation: Social media posts have no legal power over election results. Election laws are enforced by governments and courts.

headline: Scientists discovered that eating chocolate daily cures the common cold.
explanation: No scientific evidence supports chocolate as a cold cure. This is health misinformation.

headline: The Great Wall of China is visible from space with the naked eye.
explanation: Multiple astronauts have confirmed the Great Wall is too narrow to be seen from orbit without aid.

headline: Lightning never strikes the same place twice.
explanation: Lightning frequently strikes the same place — tall structures like the Empire State Building are hit dozens of times a year.

headline: The European Union has a single elected president who runs all member states.
explanation: The EU has multiple presidencies (Commission, Council, Parliament); none 'runs' the member states.

headline: Antibiotics are an effective treatment for the common cold and flu.
explanation: Antibiotics treat bacterial infections; colds and flu are viral. Misuse drives antibiotic resistance.

headline: The UN can deploy peacekeepers to any country without that country's consent.
explanation: UN peacekeeping requires consent of the host country and a Security Council mandate.

headline: Vaccines have been scientifically proven to cause autism.
explanation: The original 1998 study was retracted for fraud; dozens of large-scale studies show no link.

headline: The Earth's climate has not measurably changed in the last 100 years.
explanation: Global average temperatures have risen ~1.1°C since 1900 according to NASA, NOAA, and IPCC datasets.

headline: Every country in the world uses the same voting age of 18.
explanation: Voting ages vary — Austria and Brazil allow voting at 16; some countries set it at 20 or 21.

headline: 5G mobile networks transmit viruses to nearby humans.
explanation: Radio waves cannot transmit biological viruses. This claim has been debunked by WHO and every public-health agency.

headline: The Sahara Desert has always been a desert for all of human history.
explanation: The 'Green Sahara' period (~11,000–5,000 years ago) saw the Sahara as grassland with lakes and rivers.
